list.title: Tools
list.description: Manage reusable tools that can be used across your workflows.
list.cardDescription: Create and manage tools for your organization
list.confirmArchive: Are you sure you want to archive this tool?

categoryDef.httpApiDescription: Make HTTP requests to external APIs
categoryDef.endCallDescription: End the call when conditions are met
categoryDef.endCallAutoDescription: End the call when either user asks to disconnect the call, or when you believe its time to end the conversation
categoryDef.transferCallDescription: Transfer the call to another phone number (Twilio only)
categoryDef.transferCallAutoDescription: Transfer the caller to another phone number when requested
categoryDef.mcpDescription: Connect a customer MCP server; its tools become available to the agent
categoryDef.nativeDescription: Built-in tools like call transfer, DTMF input

endCallReasonDefault: The reason for ending the call (e.g., 'voicemail_detected', 'issue_resolved', 'customer_requested')

create.nameRequired: Please enter a name for the tool
create.mcpUrlRequired: Please enter the MCP server URL
create.mcpUrlInvalid: MCP server URL must start with http:// or https://
create.dialogDescription: Create a new tool that can be used in your workflows.
create.toolNameHint: Use a descriptive name, like "Get Weather using API" for a tool that fetches weather
create.descriptionHint: Provide a description which makes it easy for LLM to understand what this tool does
create.credentialDescription: Select a credential for authenticating with the MCP server, or leave empty for no auth.
create.toolsFilterHint: Comma-separated list of tool names to allow. Leave empty to expose all tools from the server.

detail.transferValidation: Please enter a valid phone number (E.164 format) or SIP endpoint (e.g., PJSIP/1234)
detail.mcpUrlRequired: Please enter the MCP server URL
detail.mcpUrlInvalid: MCP server URL must start with http:// or https://
detail.paramNameRequired: All parameters must have a name
detail.presetParamRequired: All preset parameters must have a name and a value
detail.codePreviewDescription: JavaScript code to make this API call
detail.mcpDescription: Configure the MCP server endpoint. Its tools become available to the agent.
detail.mcpDescriptionHint: Provide a description which makes it easy for LLM to understand what this tool does
detail.mcpDescriptionPlaceholder: What does this MCP server provide?
detail.credentialDescription: Select a credential for authenticating with the MCP server, or leave empty for no auth.
detail.toolsFilterHint: Comma-separated list of tool names to allow. Leave empty to expose all tools from the server.

builtin.calculatorSubtitle: Built-in calculator for arithmetic operations. No additional configuration needed.
builtin.descriptionHint: Provide a description which makes it easy for LLM to understand what this tool does

endCall.title: End Call Configuration
endCall.description: Configure the behavior when the call ends
endCall.toolNameHint: A descriptive name for this tool
endCall.descriptionHint: Helps the LLM understand when to use this tool
endCall.descriptionPlaceholder: When should the AI end the call?
endCall.captureReasonHint: When enabled, the AI will provide a reason for ending the call. The reason will be set as the call disposition and added to call tags for analytics.
endCall.reasonDescriptionHint: Instructions shown to the AI for what kind of reason to provide
endCall.reasonPlaceholder: e.g., The reason for ending the call (e.g., 'voicemail_detected', 'issue_resolved', 'customer_requested')
endCall.goodbyeMessageHint: Choose whether to play a message before disconnecting
endCall.noMessageHint: End the call immediately without any message
endCall.customMessageHint: Play a custom message before disconnecting
endCall.customMessagePlaceholder: e.g., Thank you for calling. Goodbye!
endCall.audioMessageHint: Play a pre-recorded audio file before disconnecting

transferCall.title: Transfer Call Configuration
transferCall.description: Configure call transfer settings. Supports phone numbers (Twilio) and SIP endpoints (Asterisk ARI).
transferCall.toolNameHint: A descriptive name for this tool
transferCall.descriptionHint: Helps the LLM understand when to use this tool
transferCall.descriptionPlaceholder: When should the AI transfer the call?
transferCall.destinationHintSip: SIP endpoint to transfer the call to (e.g., PJSIP/1234 or SIP/extension@domain.com)
transferCall.destinationHintPhone: Phone number to transfer the call to (E.164 format with country code)
transferCall.sipValidation: Please enter a valid SIP endpoint (e.g., PJSIP/1234 or SIP/extension@domain.com)
transferCall.phoneValidation: Please enter a valid phone number in E.164 format (e.g., +1234567890)
transferCall.preTransferMessageHint: Choose whether to play a message before transferring
transferCall.noMessageHint: Transfer the call immediately without any message
transferCall.customMessageHint: Play a custom message before transferring
transferCall.customMessagePlaceholder: e.g., Please hold while I transfer your call.
transferCall.audioMessageHint: Play a pre-recorded audio file before transferring
transferCall.timeoutHint: Maximum time to wait for destination to answer (5-120 seconds)

httpApi.title: Tool Configuration
httpApi.description: Configure the HTTP API endpoint and request settings
httpApi.toolNameHint: Use a descriptive name, like "Get Weather using API" for a tool that fetches weather
httpApi.descriptionHint: Provide a description which makes it easy for LLM to understand what this tool does
httpApi.customMessageHint: Optional message the AI will speak or play before executing this tool.
httpApi.customMessagePlaceholder: e.g., Let me check that for you, one moment please.
httpApi.llmParametersHint: Define the parameters that the LLM will provide when calling this tool. These will be sent as JSON body for POST/PUT/PATCH or as URL query params for GET/DELETE.
httpApi.presetParametersHintBefore: Add values that Dograh should inject at runtime. These are not exposed to the LLM and can use workflow templates like {template} or fixed literals.
httpApi.customHeadersHint: Add custom headers to include in the request (optional)
